decisions.decision: DetectOutliers takes precomputed simMatrix — not called from Analyze()
decisions.rationale: LexRank matrix only exists inside summarizer; adding it to Analyze would require circular import or refactor. Defer wiring until Phase 7 plan.
Low FP tolerance; security research docs about injection would trigger their own patterns

decisions.decision: golang.org/x/text/unicode/norm for NFKC — already transitive dep via go-readability
decisions.rationale: No new module needed; upgraded from v0.22 to v0.36

decisions.decision: Hook spec doc triggered its own injection detector hook
decisions.rationale: Expected behavior — 07-SPEC.md contains injection examples for documentation. Hook correctly flagged it as legitimate security doc.

next_action: 1. Commit 06-VERIFICATION.md. 2. Update README with injection defense section (user + technical). 3. Wire DetectOutliers into LexRank summarizer. 4. Create formal Phase 7 GSD plan.
context_notes: Phase 6 fully implemented and verified (automated). Phase 7 started mid-session based on user request for injection defense. Core packages shipped and tested. The outlier detection (DetectOutliers) is implemented in internal/detector but not yet wired into the LexRank similarity matrix pipeline — that requires passing the matrix out of lexrank.go or refactoring Summarize() to return it alongside results. The sanitizer and pattern/encoding detectors are fully wired via CLI flags.
